arts.sum: Manchester City make the trip to Leyton Orient on Saturday lunchtime in the FA Cup’s fourth round. Pep Guardiola’s side take on the League One promotion hopefuls at Brisbane Road bidding to take a step closer to Wembley. arts.ttl: Man City predicted lineup vs Leyton Orient
arts.sum: Manchester City are seeking safe passage to the FA Cup fifth round on Saturday when they face League One outfit Leyton Orient. Having fired eight past Salford City in the third round, the Cityzens will be aiming to pour similar misery on Leyton Orient this weekend as they attempt to reach a third successive FA Cup final.

arts.ttl: Matheus Nunes: Why Man City cannot win Champions League with makeshift right-back who has "no defensive instincts at all"

arts.ttl: From crisis club to ‘doing a Brentford’: Leyton Orient upbeat before City test
arts.sum: Chairman Nigel Travis welcomes the Premier League champions in the FA Cup with grand plans for the future “I remember going to Maine Road in 1964 with my dad and we got walloped six-nothing,” recalls Nigel Travis. “I think it’s the only time I’ve ever seen us play them.” The Leyton Orient chairman’s account of watching his club face Manchester City is rare among fans of the League One team – the teams have not met since a second-tier 2-2 draw in 1966. Fortunes have diverged since but Orient welcome City to east London on Saturday in upbeat mood and at something of a crossroads. After seven and a half years of steady upward progress since Travis’s consortium took over a club in ruins after relegation to the National League, the chairman has been soliciting fresh investment. A new stadium nearby is being considered and talk has been afoot of aping their former lower division London rivals and “doing a Brentford”. The manager, Richie Wellens, has spoken of “not being ‘little Leyton Orient’ any more”. Continue reading...
